mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound, the discount rate applied to equity cash flows remains structurally low, supporting a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 prices over a quarterly horizon regardless of short-term noise. This is an always-on carry effect of accommodative policy.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 60d SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window during zero-rate periods, or positive-return frequency in zero-rate regimes is not statistically higher than the unconditional base rate (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals accelerating labor market healing, which raises consensus earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as investors price in a stronger consumer base.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE fell from higher pandemic peak to 6.0% by March 2021
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following trigger events are negative or statistically indistinguishable from unconditional returns (t-test p > 0.05) across at least two distinct historical recovery episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that is not decomposable into either series alone; joint strength signals durable aggregate demand momentum that pushes S&P 500 returns higher over the next month, whereas either series rising alone is a weaker, noisier signal.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 5% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 619,105M and HOUST at 1,421k units as of early 2021 vintage
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances produce forward 20d returns no better than either single-series trigger in isolation (interaction term coefficient not significant at 5% level in walk-forward regression).

mechanism_narrative: During a post-pandemic-shock recovery regime, a sustained rebound in INDPRO signals restoration of production capacity and supply chain normalization, which supports corporate margin recovery and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon. Outside recovery regimes, INDPRO changes carry weaker predictive power because capacity utilization is already near trend.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth turns positive after prior contraction
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO recovering from 2020 pandemic trough toward 98.4-105.6 range by March 2021
failure_condition: In non-recovery (mid or late expansion) regimes, the same INDPRO rebound trigger produces forward 60d returns with Rank IC below 0.03, or in the current recovery regime returns are not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation accelerates past a threshold consistent with overheating concerns, markets begin pricing in earlier Fed tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL rising toward 264.96 in early 2021 amid reopening demand
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following CPI threshold breaches remain positive or flat with statistical significance (p < 0.05 in favor of positive returns) across multiple historical inflation-acceleration episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects continuous hiring momentum that supports near-term consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, producing a modest positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week. This is a persistent, always-on employment growth effect.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS rising to 144,232k (revised) by March 2021 vintage
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS monthly growth and forward 5d SPX returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or sign hit-rate does not exceed 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in GDPC1 (real output growth) and RSAFS (retail sales growth) jointly indicates that aggregate demand expansion is broad-based across both production and consumption channels, a combination that more reliably signals durable economic expansion than either series alone, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 3% AND RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 revised sharply upward to 20,791.9B reflecting strong recovery growth
failure_condition: Joint GDPC1-RSAFS trigger events fail to produce forward 60d returns significantly better than the RSAFS-only or GDPC1-only signal (interaction coefficient not significant at 5% level), or GDPC1 revision volatility invalidates the trigger's real-time reliability.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where FEDFUNDS is pinned at the zero lower bound while CPIAUCSL inflation is simultaneously accelerating, markets face heightened uncertainty about the timing of policy liftoff, generating volatility and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as investors discount the risk of a hawkish surprise; outside this specific joint regime, rising inflation alone has weaker effect since rate hike risk is not imminent.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.07% while CPIAUCSL rising toward 264.96 as of March 2021
regime_hint: zero lower bound with rising inflation risk
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns during joint zero-rate/high-inflation trigger periods are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) relative to baseline, or the effect disappears once FEDFUNDS lifts off zero, invalidating the regime-specific claim.
